good that it builds, but does it actually run?

Another important/interestign question - can gcc and clang built versions interoperate?
Patch Set 15: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 7f1aa5b66b247f339a9e7c25415a9a5dd272763c by Oleg Drokin

Patch Set 16:

> Patch Set 15:
> 
> good that it builds, but does it actually run?
> 
> Another important/interestign question - can gcc and clang built versions interoperate?

Seems to run fine. I've been using Clang/LLVM primarily for dev purposes for the past 5+ months. My testing hasn't been comprehensive, but the sanity tests I have run pass. I haven't noticed anything unusual.

GCC and Clang versions can interoperate. I've connected a Clang built client to GCC built servers and run some simple tests.

The main annoyance of building Lustre with Clang is having to build the kernel with Clang beforehand.

Now that this landed, I'll probably try some more comprehensive testing/experimentation.

parents.subject: LU-13343 gss: no sec flavor on loopback connection

commit.subject: LU-16518 build: llvm/clang support
commit.message:
LU-16518 build: llvm/clang support

Other projects, notably Linux, have build support for LLVM and
Clang via special environment variables. This is implemented
for Lustre, in the style of:

https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/latest/kbuild/llvm.html

Instances in which GCC is explicitly called are replaced by the
use of $CC. The proper environment variables as passed to make
invocations as needed.

All checks which influence global compiler and toolchain settings
are collected in 'config/lustre-toolchain.m4'.

A configure option is added to disable the strict error flags that
are passes to the C compiler by default. CFLAGS and EXTRA_CFLAGS
are made to work in the typical way. Having fine grained control
over compiler options makes experimenting with Clang smoother.

Some compile checks in 'lustre-core.m4' have been improved by using
unused variables and explicitly setting the compile flag to be used
during the test.

This also sets the execute bit on autogen.sh.

Tested with:
Linux (mainline) - 5.15.94
openZFS - 2.1.99
Lustre (latest master) - 2.15.55
CentOS - 8.5
Clang (default on CentOS) - 12.0.1

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
